prompt_text: respond to customer
handle complaint
help with ticket

Draft an empathetic response to a customer whose order arrived damaged, acknowledge the frustration, offer a 20% discount on next purchase plus free replacement shipped via express, include a case number and expected delivery timeline of 3-5 business days

Write a de-escalation response for an enterprise customer threatening to cancel their $120K annual contract due to 3 consecutive days of API outages. Acknowledge impact on their business, outline the root cause (database failover issue), detail our remediation plan with specific timeline, and offer SLA credits plus a direct line to our VP of Engineering
write a FAQ

Create a comprehensive FAQ document for our enterprise SSO integration: cover SAML 2.0 setup, SCIM provisioning, IdP-initiated vs SP-initiated flows, group mapping, MFA requirements, common error codes with solutions, and a troubleshooting decision tree for the top 10 issues reported by customers
customer onboarding email

Draft a welcome email sequence for new enterprise customers (5 emails over 14 days): Day 1 welcome + account setup guide, Day 3 first integration tutorial, Day 7 advanced features overview, Day 10 check-in from CSM with meeting scheduling link, Day 14 ROI tracking setup. Each email: subject line, body, CTA
cancel request response

Write a retention-focused response to a customer requesting cancellation: acknowledge their right to cancel, ask 3 specific questions about their pain points, offer a 60-minute consultation with our solutions architect to address their concerns, and present 3 alternative options (plan downgrade, pause, custom plan). Tone: empathetic, not pushy
bug report response

Write a technical bug acknowledgment response: confirm we've reproduced the issue (data export fails for reports >10K rows), explain the root cause (memory limit in export service), provide a workaround (paginated export via API), share our fix timeline (patch in v4.2.1, ETA next Tuesday), and offer to notify them when deployed

Write a service recovery email after a major outage: 4-hour downtime impacting all customers. Include a sincere apology, timeline of events, root cause (misconfigured load balancer during routine maintenance), steps taken to prevent recurrence, SLA credit details, and a link to our updated status page with real-time monitoring

Create a customer health scoring model: define 8 signals (login frequency, feature adoption breadth, support ticket sentiment, NPS score, contract utilization, expansion signals, executive engagement, integration depth), weight each signal, create 4 health categories (Thriving, Healthy, At Risk, Critical), and define playbooks for each category
knowledge base article

Write a knowledge base article for setting up webhook integrations: prerequisites, step-by-step configuration with screenshots placeholders, payload format with JSON examples, retry logic explanation, signature verification code samples in Python/Node/Java, common error troubleshooting, and rate limit guidelines

Draft a quarterly business review (QBR) presentation outline for an enterprise customer: cover platform usage metrics, value delivered (quantified), feature adoption vs plan, support experience summary, product roadmap alignment with their needs, expansion opportunities, and mutual success plan for next quarter
reply to the customer nicely

Write a response to a customer who accidentally deleted their production database asking for recovery: express urgency-matched empathy, explain our backup policy (hourly snapshots, 30-day retention), outline the recovery process (point-in-time restore, estimated 2-hour RTO), ask them to confirm the desired recovery timestamp, and proactively suggest enabling deletion protection afterward
help with strategy
business plan
something about growth

Create a go-to-market plan for launching our AI product in the DACH region: market analysis with TAM/SAM, competitive landscape (3 local and 3 global competitors), pricing strategy adapted for European market, channel partner strategy, regulatory considerations (GDPR, AI Act), 6-month timeline with milestones, budget allocation for $500K

Develop a 3-year product strategy for our data platform: define vision and north star metric, identify 5 strategic pillars with quarterly OKRs, map competitive moats, outline build-vs-buy decisions for 8 capability areas, create a technology radar for emerging trends (vector databases, real-time ML, data mesh), and define success metrics per year
M&A stuff

Create an M&A evaluation framework for acquiring a data observability startup: define 12 evaluation criteria (technology fit, team quality, customer overlap, revenue multiple, integration complexity, etc.), weight each criterion, score 3 target companies, and produce a recommendation memo with financial analysis showing 3-year NPV scenarios
pricing model

Design a consumption-based pricing model for our cloud data platform: define 5 pricing dimensions (compute, storage, data transfer, API calls, users), model elasticity curves for each, create 3 packaging tiers (Starter, Business, Enterprise), run Monte Carlo simulation for revenue impact across 4 customer segments, and compare vs our current seat-based model
investor deck

Create a Series C investor deck outline (15 slides): slide 1 vision statement, slide 2 market opportunity ($50B TAM), slides 3-4 product and technology moat, slide 5 business model, slides 6-7 traction metrics (ARR $25M, 150% NRR, 200+ customers), slide 8 competitive landscape with positioning matrix, slides 9-10 GTM strategy, slide 11 team, slides 12-13 financials (3-year projections), slide 14 use of funds ($100M raise), slide 15 ask and timeline
risk assessment

Conduct a comprehensive risk assessment for our platform expansion into regulated industries (healthcare, finance): identify 20 risks across compliance, technical, operational, and market categories. For each risk: likelihood (1-5), impact (1-5), risk score, mitigation strategy, residual risk, and owner. Create a heat map visualization and prioritized action plan for the top 5 risks
OKRs please

Define Q2 2026 OKRs for our engineering organization (150 people, 12 teams): 4 objectives aligned with company strategy, each with 3-4 measurable key results. Include dependencies between teams, resource allocation considerations, stretch vs committed targets, and a scoring rubric. Objectives should cover: platform reliability, developer productivity, AI feature delivery, and technical debt reduction
partnership strategy

Develop a technology partnership strategy: identify 10 potential partners across cloud providers, data tools, and system integrators. For each: strategic rationale, integration complexity, revenue opportunity, competitive implications. Create a partnership tier framework (Technology, Solution, Strategic), define mutual commitments per tier, and build a 12-month engagement roadmap with quarterly milestones
organizational design

Design an organizational structure for scaling our engineering team from 50 to 200 people over 18 months: define team topology (stream-aligned, platform, enabling, complicated-subsystem teams), reporting structure, hiring plan by quarter, onboarding program, and culture preservation strategy. Include span of control guidelines and cross-team coordination mechanisms

Create a data monetization strategy: inventory our 15 data assets, assess monetization potential (direct licensing, insights-as-a-service, data-enhanced features, marketplace), evaluate privacy implications per asset, design 3 revenue models with 5-year projections, and create a phased rollout plan starting with lowest-risk highest-value opportunities

Build a customer success playbook for enterprise accounts: define lifecycle stages (onboarding, adoption, expansion, renewal), key activities per stage, health score triggers for intervention, escalation paths, QBR templates, and success metrics. Include role-specific guidelines for CSMs, TAMs, and Solutions Architects

Create an internal training curriculum for support engineers: 8-week program covering product deep-dive, troubleshooting methodology, SQL/API debugging skills, customer communication best practices, escalation criteria, and certification exam. Include hands-on labs for the top 20 support scenarios with expected resolution paths

Write an incident communication template set: initial acknowledgment (within 15 min), hourly updates during incident, resolution notification, and post-mortem summary. Each template should have placeholders for severity level, affected services, user impact, and ETA. Include tone guidelines for P1 vs P2 vs P3 incidents
implement caching

Design and implement a multi-layer caching strategy for our API: L1 in-memory cache (LRU, 100MB, 5-min TTL) for hot paths, L2 Redis cache (1GB, 1-hour TTL) for session and user data, cache invalidation via pub/sub on data mutations, cache stampede prevention with probabilistic early expiration, and monitoring dashboard with hit rates per layer and endpoint

Build a feature flag system from scratch: support boolean, percentage, and user-segment based flags. Include a React SDK for client-side evaluation, Python SDK for server-side, admin API for flag management, audit log for changes, and gradual rollout capability with automatic rollback if error rate exceeds threshold

Create a chaos engineering framework for our microservices: define 10 failure injection scenarios (network latency, pod crash, disk full, CPU spike, DNS failure, etc.), implement using Litmus Chaos, create a game day runbook, define blast radius limits, and build a resilience scorecard that tracks MTTR across quarterly chaos experiments

Develop a capacity planning model for our SaaS infrastructure: input current metrics (CPU, memory, storage, network utilization by service), growth projections (20% QoQ), seasonal patterns, and cost per resource. Output: quarterly capacity needs, infrastructure cost forecast, scaling recommendations, and reserved vs on-demand instance optimization
analyze our competitors
write about AI

Write a thought leadership article on 'The 5 Stages of Enterprise AI Maturity' for our company blog: from ad-hoc experimentation to industrialized ML. Each stage should have a description, typical company profile, key challenges, recommended tools/practices, and metrics to know when to advance. Include real-world examples (anonymized) and a self-assessment checklist
create documentation

Write comprehensive API documentation for our ML model serving platform: architecture overview with diagrams, authentication (API key + OAuth2), 8 endpoint references with request/response examples, Python/JavaScript/curl code samples, error handling guide, rate limits, versioning policy, migration guide from v1 to v2, and a quickstart tutorial that deploys a model in under 5 minutes

Create a vendor evaluation scorecard for selecting a log management solution: compare Datadog, Splunk, and Elastic across 15 criteria (ingestion volume, query performance, alerting, dashboards, integrations, compliance features, cost at 1TB/day, SLA, support quality, etc.), weight criteria by priority, calculate weighted scores, and recommend with justification
write a training plan

Design a comprehensive data governance framework: define data classification levels (public, internal, confidential, restricted), access control policies per level, data quality standards, retention schedules, privacy compliance mapping (GDPR Art. 5, CCPA), stewardship roles and responsibilities, and an implementation roadmap with quick wins in the first 90 days
help me with something

Write a competitive battle card for sales: our platform vs Snowflake. Structure: elevator pitch (30 sec), top 3 differentiators with proof points, common customer objections with rebuttals, trap-setting discovery questions, pricing comparison scenarios, recent win stories, and red flags that indicate a competitive deal

Build a sentiment analysis pipeline using Delta Live Tables: ingest tweets from Kafka, clean and normalize text, run inference using a fine-tuned DistilBERT model via MLflow, aggregate sentiment by brand and time window, materialize gold tables for BI dashboards, and include data quality expectations at each layer
do AI things
